name: Project ATLAS — ATT&CK Coverage
description: MITRE ATT&CK heatmap for Project ATLAS SOC Tier 1/2 simulation. Techniques simulated and detected during a 5-day cloud + endpoint attack simulation on Azure/M365 (July 2026). Red = simulated but NOT detected (telemetry gap). Green = simulated AND detected. Yellow = detected by cloud identity only (partial coverage).

techniques.comment: SIMULATED — NOT DETECTED. Hydra RDP brute force attack from Kali (10.0.2.15) against DESKTOP-TRF9U79 soclab-test account. Expected to fire ATLAS rule 4.1 (SecurityEvent 4625 spike). Actual result: SecurityEvent table = 0 records (AMA pipeline failure). No Sentinel alert generated.

SIMULATED — NOT DETECTED. Hydra password guessing via RDP (TCP 3389, NAT port 33389). ~100 attempts before successful logon. EventID 4625 should appear in SecurityEvent table. AMA pipeline failure caused 0 records in SecurityEvent. Detection gap confirmed via KQL: SecurityEvent | summarize count() = 0.

SIMULATED — NOT DETECTED. Base64-encoded PowerShell command executed post-RDP access. Expected to fire ATLAS rule 4.2 (Sysmon EventID 1 -enc pattern). Actual result: Sysmon Event table = 0 records (AMA pipeline failure).

SIMULATED — NOT DETECTED. PowerShell -enc <base64> executed on DESKTOP-TRF9U79. Sysmon EventID 1 process create should capture command line. AMA failure = 0 records in Event table. ATLAS rule 4.2 did not fire.

SIMULATED — NOT DETECTED. Base64 encoding used to obfuscate PowerShell command (paired with T1059.001). Sysmon command-line logging would capture the -enc flag and encoded blob. AMA failure = no visibility.

SIMULATED — PARTIALLY TRACKED. M365 Attack Simulation Training (Credential Harvest template). 1 click, 1 credential submission captured in Microsoft 365 Defender Attack simulation training reports. Not forwarded to Log Analytics / Sentinel — no huntable telemetry in SIEM. Partial credit: event occurred and was captured in M365 Defender portal.

SIMULATED — PARTIALLY TRACKED. Spearphishing Link template: fake Microsoft login page. User clicked and submitted credentials. Tracked in M365 Attack Simulation Training portal. Not a real incident in Defender XDR (simulation is flagged safe). No SIEM telemetry.

DETECTED — FULL COVERAGE. Valid cloud account (soc-tor-test@VANPASSSC200.onmicrosoft.com) used to authenticate via Tor exit nodes. Detected by Entra ID Protection (Anonymous IP address + Anomalous Token). Correlated into Incident ID 2 by Defender XDR. Analyst classified True positive. User confirmed compromised.

DETECTED — FULL COVERAGE. Cloud account sign-in via Tor anonymizing proxy. Entra ID Protection fired 5 Anonymous IP address risk detections and 3 Anomalous Token detections. Classified true positive. Conditional Access policy created to block future anonymous IP sign-ins.

DETECTED — FULL COVERAGE. Tor Browser used as anonymizing proxy to sign in to M365/Entra ID. Source IPs: 2a03:4000:46:197:b434:d3ff:fe68:d9e1 (Karlsruhe, DE), 2a0f:df00:0:255::194 (Sweden). Entra ID Protection identified as anonymous IP (Tor exit node).

DETECTED — FULL COVERAGE. Multi-hop proxy via Tor network. Exit nodes in Germany and Sweden. Entra ID Protection anonymous IP detection fired on each successful sign-in. 5 total detections across 2 Tor exit IPs.

legendItems.label: Simulated — Partially tracked (M365 Defender only, no SIEM)
Simulated — DETECTED (Entra ID Protection + Defender XDR)

metadata.name: Key Finding
metadata.value: Endpoint telemetry gap (AMA) left 4/6 techniques blind. Cloud identity detection (Entra ID Protection) performed as expected.
